title: NA
text: (Strikethrough points applicable.) comment contains description changes justifications, relevant issues linked. Write unit tests new features, bug fixes, code changes. Update docs API changes (roxygen2 comments, vignettes, readme, etc.). Update NEWS.md short description user-facing changes reference PR number. Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html Run devtools::check() locally fix notes, warnings, errors.

title: functracer: Introductory vignette
code: library(functracer)  result <- analyze_dependencies(   entry_script = "path/to/main.R",   package_dir = "path/to/package-root" )  result  output <- trace_functions(   entry_script = "path/to/main.R",   package_dir = "path/to/package-root",   output_format = "svg",   output_dir = ".",   output_prefix = "analysis" )  output$output_path

title: functracer: Introductory vignette
text: example creates minimal package script temp folder.
code: library(functracer)  work_dir <- tempfile("functracer-demo-") dir.create(work_dir)  demo_pkg <- file.path(work_dir, "demoPkg") dir.create(demo_pkg) dir.create(file.path(demo_pkg, "R"))  writeLines(   c(     "Package: demoPkg",     "Version: 0.0.1",     "Title: Demo Package",     "Description: Minimal package for functracer vignette.",     "License: MIT"   ),   file.path(demo_pkg, "DESCRIPTION") )  writeLines(   c("export(core_fn)", "export(helper_fn)"),   file.path(demo_pkg, "NAMESPACE") )  writeLines(   c(     "core_fn <- function(x) {",     "  helper_fn(x)",     "}",     "",     "helper_fn <- function(x) {",     "  x + 1",     "}"   ),   file.path(demo_pkg, "R", "functions.R") )  entry_script <- file.path(work_dir, "main.R") writeLines(   c(     "run <- function(v) {",     "  core_fn(v)",     "}",     "",     "run(3)"   ),   entry_script )  result <- analyze_dependencies(   entry_script = entry_script,   package_dir = demo_pkg )  result #>    function dep_type hop_depth                             call_path  source #> 1   core_fn   direct         1                     main.R -> core_fn demoPkg #> 2       run   direct         1                         main.R -> run  main.R #> 3   core_fn indirect         2              main.R -> run -> core_fn demoPkg #> 4 helper_fn indirect         2        main.R -> core_fn -> helper_fn demoPkg #> 5 helper_fn indirect         3 main.R -> run -> core_fn -> helper_fn demoPkg #>   is_exported #> 1        TRUE #> 2       FALSE #> 3        TRUE #> 4        TRUE #> 5        TRUE

title: functracer: Introductory vignette
code: output <- trace_functions(   entry_script = entry_script,   package_dir = demo_pkg,   output_format = "csv",   output_dir = work_dir,   output_prefix = "demo" ) #> Dependency analysis complete #> Format: csv #> Output: /tmp/RtmpjJxqNR/functracer-demo-1bdb59de484f/demo_dependencies.csv  out <- read.csv(output$output_path, check.names = FALSE) out #>    function dep_type hop_depth                             call_path  source #> 1   core_fn   direct         1                     main.R -> core_fn demoPkg #> 2       run   direct         1                         main.R -> run  main.R #> 3   core_fn indirect         2              main.R -> run -> core_fn demoPkg #> 4 helper_fn indirect         2        main.R -> core_fn -> helper_fn demoPkg #> 5 helper_fn indirect         3 main.R -> run -> core_fn -> helper_fn demoPkg #>   is_exported #> 1        TRUE #> 2       FALSE #> 3        TRUE #> 4        TRUE #> 5        TRUE

title: functracer: Introductory vignette
text: Use trace_release_impact() want check whether functions traced dependency graph changed tags. script_affected TRUE least one traced dependency comes R source file changed inferred previous_tag (explicit previous_tag) release_tag.
code: release_result <- trace_release_impact(   entry_script = "path/to/main.R",   repository = "https://github.com/owner/package.git",   release_tag = "v1.2.0",   package_subdir = "packages/myPkg" )  release_result$script_affected release_result$changed_dependencies

title: functracer: Introductory vignette
text: dep_type = direct means called entry script. dep_type = indirect means reached functions. Larger hop_depth means farther entry script. call_path gives exact chain used reach function. enough answer: “change function X, workflows affected?”

what: Trace Function Dependencies in R Code
title: Trace Function Dependencies in R Code
text: Trace direct transitive function dependencies R script target package source tree. https://ccbr.github.io/functracer

title: Trace Function Dependencies in R Code
text: Extract direct function calls R script. Build package function adjacency R/ source files. Traverse transitive dependencies retain call paths. Compare tagged GitHub release previous tag flag traced dependencies changed. Export dependency artifacts CSV, JSON, SVG. Supports regular function assignments S7 generics/methods.

title: Trace Function Dependencies in R Code
Trace Function Dependencies in R Code
text: See introductory vignette detailed introduction.

title: Trace Function Dependencies in R Code
code: library(functracer)  result <- analyze_dependencies(   entry_script = "path/to/main.R",   package_dir = "path/to/package-root" )  result  output <- trace_functions(   entry_script = "path/to/main.R",   package_dir = "path/to/package-root",   output_format = "json",   output_dir = ".",   output_prefix = "analysis" )  output$output_path  release_result <- trace_release_impact(   entry_script = "path/to/main.R",   repository = "https://github.com/owner/package.git",   release_tag = "v1.2.0",   package_subdir = "packages/myPkg" )  release_result$script_affected release_result$changed_dependencies

title: Trace Function Dependencies in R Code
text: commands source checkouts repository (call inst/scripts/functracer.R directly). CLI currently assumes package lives repository root release-analysis mode.
code: Rscript inst/scripts/functracer.R \   --entry path/to/main.R \   --package-dir path/to/package-root \   --output-format json \   --output-dir . \   --prefix analysis  Rscript inst/scripts/functracer.R \   --entry path/to/main.R \   --repo-url https://github.com/owner/package.git \   --release-tag v1.2.0 \   --previous-tag v1.1.0 \   --output-format json \   --output-dir . \   --prefix release-analysis

title: Trace Function Dependencies in R Code
text: CSV/JSON includes: function dep_type (direct indirect) hop_depth call_path source is_exported

title: Trace Function Dependencies in R Code
text: CSV/JSON includes local fields plus: source_file source_file_changed release_tag previous_tag

what: Analyze Direct and Transitive R Function Dependencies — analyze_dependencies
title: Analyze Direct and Transitive R Function Dependencies — analyze_dependencies
text: Build dependency map starting entry script tracing target package source directory.

title: Analyze Direct and Transitive R Function Dependencies — analyze_dependencies
Analyze Direct and Transitive R Function Dependencies — analyze_dependencies
text: entry_script Path entry R script. package_dir Path package root directory containing R/ NAMESPACE. package_name Optional package name. NULL, inferred DESCRIPTION.

title: Analyze Direct and Transitive R Function Dependencies — analyze_dependencies
text: data frame one row per traced function columns: function, dep_type, hop_depth, call_path, source, is_exported.

title: Analyze Direct and Transitive R Function Dependencies — analyze_dependencies
code: temp_root <- tempfile("functracer-example-") dir.create(temp_root) pkg_dir <- file.path(temp_root, "demoPkg") dir.create(pkg_dir) dir.create(file.path(pkg_dir, "R")) writeLines(   c(     "Package: demoPkg",     "Version: 0.0.1",     "Title: Demo Package",     "Description: Demo package for examples.",     "License: MIT"   ),   file.path(pkg_dir, "DESCRIPTION") ) writeLines("export(core_fn)", file.path(pkg_dir, "NAMESPACE")) writeLines(   c(     "core_fn <- function(x) {",     "  x + 1",     "}"   ),   file.path(pkg_dir, "R", "core.R") ) entry_script <- file.path(temp_root, "main.R") writeLines("core_fn(3)", entry_script) analyze_dependencies(entry_script = entry_script, package_dir = pkg_dir) #>   function dep_type hop_depth         call_path  source is_exported #> 1  core_fn   direct         1 main.R -> core_fn demoPkg        TRUE

what: Trace Functions and Write One Output Format — trace_functions
title: Trace Functions and Write One Output Format — trace_functions
text: Trace Functions Write One Output Format

title: Trace Functions and Write One Output Format — trace_functions
code: trace_functions(   entry_script,   package_dir,   package_name = NULL,   output_dir = ".",   output_prefix = NULL,   output_format = c("csv", "json", "svg") )

title: Trace Functions and Write One Output Format — trace_functions
text: entry_script Path entry R script. package_dir Path package root directory containing R/ NAMESPACE. package_name Optional package name. NULL, inferred DESCRIPTION. output_dir Directory output artifacts written. output_prefix Prefix output files. Defaults entry script stem. output_format Output file format. One "csv", "json", "svg".

title: Trace Functions and Write One Output Format — trace_functions
text: Invisibly returns list : output_path (artifact path), output_format (selected format), dependencies (data frame returned analyze_dependencies()).

title: Trace Functions and Write One Output Format — trace_functions
code: temp_root <- tempfile("functracer-example-") dir.create(temp_root) pkg_dir <- file.path(temp_root, "demoPkg") dir.create(pkg_dir) dir.create(file.path(pkg_dir, "R")) writeLines(   c(     "Package: demoPkg",     "Version: 0.0.1",     "Title: Demo Package",     "Description: Demo package for examples.",     "License: MIT"   ),   file.path(pkg_dir, "DESCRIPTION") ) writeLines("export(core_fn)", file.path(pkg_dir, "NAMESPACE")) writeLines(   c(     "core_fn <- function(x) {",     "  x + 1",     "}"   ),   file.path(pkg_dir, "R", "core.R") ) entry_script <- file.path(temp_root, "main.R") writeLines("core_fn(3)", entry_script) out <- trace_functions(   entry_script = entry_script,   package_dir = pkg_dir,   output_dir = temp_root,   output_format = "json" ) #> Dependency analysis complete #> Format: json #> Output: /tmp/RtmpXipNcC/functracer-example-1b0610f73619/main_dependencies.json file.exists(out$output_path) #> [1] TRUE

what: Trace dependencies against a tagged package release — trace_release_impact
title: Trace dependencies against a tagged package release — trace_release_impact
text: Clone git repository, check requested release tag, compare previous tag, identify traced dependencies whose source files changed.

title: Trace dependencies against a tagged package release — trace_release_impact
code: trace_release_impact(   entry_script,   repository,   release_tag,   package_name = NULL,   package_subdir = NULL,   previous_tag = NULL,   output_dir = ".",   output_prefix = NULL,   output_format = c("csv", "json", "svg") )

title: Trace dependencies against a tagged package release — trace_release_impact
text: entry_script Path entry R script. repository GitHub repository URL local git repository path containing tagged package release. release_tag Release tag analyze. package_name Optional package name override. package_subdir Optional repository subdirectory containing package. previous_tag Optional previous tag override. output_dir Directory output artifacts written. output_prefix Prefix output files. Defaults entry script stem. output_format Output file format. One "csv", "json", "svg".

title: Trace dependencies against a tagged package release — trace_release_impact
text: Invisibly returns list : output_path, output_format, dependencies, changed_dependencies, changed_files, release_tag, previous_tag, repository, script_affected.

title: Trace dependencies against a tagged package release — trace_release_impact
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ temp_root <- tempfile("functracer-release-example-") dir.create(temp_root)  repo_dir <- file.path(temp_root, "demo-repo") dir.create(repo_dir) dir.create(file.path(repo_dir, "R"))  writeLines(   c(     "Package: demoPkg",     "Version: 0.0.1",     "Title: Demo Package",     "Description: Demo package for release examples.",     "License: MIT"   ),   file.path(repo_dir, "DESCRIPTION") ) writeLines("export(core_fn)", file.path(repo_dir, "NAMESPACE")) writeLines(   c(     "core_fn <- function(x) {",     "  x + 1",     "}"   ),   file.path(repo_dir, "R", "core.R") )  old_wd <- getwd() setwd(repo_dir) on.exit(setwd(old_wd), add = TRUE) system("git init --quiet") system("git config user.email 'example@example.com'") system("git config user.name 'Example User'") system("git add DESCRIPTION NAMESPACE R/core.R") system("git commit -m 'initial release' --quiet") system("git tag v0.1.0")  writeLines(   c(     "core_fn <- function(x) {",     "  x + 2",     "}"   ),   file.path(repo_dir, "R", "core.R") ) system("git add R/core.R") system("git commit -m 'update core' --quiet") system("git tag v0.2.0")  entry_script <- file.path(temp_root, "main.R") writeLines("core_fn(3)", entry_script)  out <- trace_release_impact(   entry_script = entry_script,   repository = repo_dir,   release_tag = "v0.2.0",   previous_tag = "v0.1.0",   output_dir = temp_root,   output_format = "csv" ) out$script_affected } # }
